data.content:
### Create Vector Bucket in Supabase Storage

Source: https://github.com/supabase/supabase/blob/master/apps/www/_blog/2025-12-01-vector-buckets.mdx

Initialize a Vector Bucket in Supabase Storage using the JavaScript SDK. This creates a managed storage tier for embeddings with S3-style durability and scale capabilities.

```javascript
import { createClient } from '@supabase/supabase-js'

const supabase = createClient('https://your-project.supabase.co', 'your-service-key')

await supabase.storage.vectors.createBucket('embeddings')
```

--------------------------------

### Initialize Storage Bucket in Supabase

Source: https://github.com/supabase/supabase/blob/master/apps/docs/content/_partials/product_management_sql_template.mdx

Inserts a storage bucket named 'Product Image' into the storage.buckets table. This bucket is used to store product images and media files associated with products in the system.

```sql
insert into storage.buckets (id, name)
  values ('Product Image', 'Product Image');
```

--------------------------------

### Create a Supabase Storage bucket

Source: https://github.com/supabase/supabase/blob/master/apps/docs/content/guides/storage/quickstart.mdx

This section demonstrates how to create a new storage bucket in Supabase. Buckets serve as distinct containers for files and folders, enabling different security and access rules. They can be created using SQL or various Supabase client libraries. Ensure bucket names adhere to AWS object key naming guidelines.

```SQL
-- Use Postgres to create a bucket.

insert into storage.buckets
  (id, name)
values
  ('avatars', 'avatars');
```

```JavaScript
// Use the JS library to create a bucket.

const { data, error } = await supabase.storage.createBucket('avatars')
```

```Dart
void main() async {
  final supabase = SupabaseClient('supabaseUrl', 'supabaseKey');

  final storageResponse = await supabase
      .storage
      .createBucket('avatars');
}
```

```Swift
try await supabase.storage.createBucket("avatars")
```

```Python
response = supabase.storage.create_bucket('avatars')
```

--------------------------------

### Create Supabase Storage Bucket with File and Mime Type Restrictions (JavaScript)

Source: https://github.com/supabase/supabase/blob/master/apps/docs/content/guides/storage/buckets/creating-buckets.mdx

This JavaScript example shows how to create a Supabase storage bucket with advanced configuration options. The 'avatars' bucket is set to public, accepts only image files (`image/*`), and has a maximum file size limit of 1MB. Files not meeting these criteria will be rejected upon upload.

```javascript
import { createClient } from '@supabase/supabase-js'
const supabase = createClient(process.env.SUPABASE_URL!, process.env.SUPABASE_KEY!)

// ---cut---
// Use the JS library to create a bucket.

const { data, error } = await supabase.storage.createBucket('avatars', {
  public: true,
  allowedMimeTypes: ['image/*'],
  fileSizeLimit: '1MB'
})
```

--------------------------------

### Download File from Supabase Storage Bucket - Multi-language

Source: https://github.com/supabase/supabase/blob/master/apps/docs/content/guides/storage/quickstart.mdx

Downloads a file from a specified Supabase Storage bucket using the storage client library. The method returns the file data and handles errors. Each language implementation uses the bucket name and file path to retrieve the file contents.

```javascript
// Use the JS library to download a file.

const { data, error } = await supabase.storage.from('avatars').download('public/avatar1.png')
```

```dart
void main() async {
  final supabase = SupabaseClient('supabaseUrl', 'supabaseKey');

  final storageResponse = await supabase
      .storage
      .from('public')
      .download('example.txt');
}
```

```swift
let response = try await supabase.storage.from("avatars").download(path: "public/avatar1.png")
```

```python
response = supabase.storage.from_('avatars').download('public/avatar1.png')
```

--------------------------------

### SQL Query to list Supabase storage buckets with total size

Source: https://github.com/supabase/supabase/blob/master/apps/docs/content/guides/platform/manage-your-usage/storage-size.mdx

This SQL query calculates and displays the total size of each storage bucket in megabytes. It aggregates the size of all objects within each bucket from the `storage.objects` table, extracts size from the `metadata` JSONB column, and orders the results by total size in descending order for easy identification of the largest buckets.

```sql
select
    bucket_id,
    (sum((metadata->>'size')::int) / 1048576.0)::numeric(10, 2) as total_size_megabyte
from
    storage.objects
group by
    bucket_id
order by
    total_size_megabyte desc;
```

--------------------------------

### Create Supabase Storage Buckets

Source: https://github.com/supabase/supabase/blob/master/apps/docs/content/guides/storage/buckets/creating-buckets.mdx

This snippet demonstrates how to create a Supabase Storage bucket named 'avatars' with public access. It includes code examples for JavaScript, SQL, Dart, Swift, and Python. For client library examples, ensure you have initialized the Supabase client with your project URL and API key.

```javascript
import { createClient } from '@supabase/supabase-js'
const supabase = createClient(process.env.SUPABASE_URL!, process.env.SUPABASE_KEY!)

// ---cut---
// Use the JS library to create a bucket.

const { data, error } = await supabase.storage.createBucket('avatars', {
  public: true // default: false
})
```

```sql
insert into storage.buckets
  (id, name, public)
values
  ('avatars', 'avatars', true);
```

```dart
void main() async {
  final supabase = SupabaseClient('supabaseUrl', 'supabaseKey');

  final storageResponse = await supabase
      .storage
      .createBucket('avatars');
}
```

```swift
try await supabase.storage.createBucket(
  "avatars",
  options: BucketOptions(public: true)
)
```

```python
supabase.storage.create_bucket(
  'avatars',
  options={"public": True}
)
```

--------------------------------

### Upload a file to Supabase Storage

Source: https://github.com/supabase/supabase/blob/master/apps/docs/content/guides/storage/quickstart.mdx

This section outlines how to upload files to an existing Supabase Storage bucket. Files can be uploaded programmatically using client libraries like JavaScript or Dart. You must specify the target bucket and the desired file path within that bucket.

```JavaScript
const avatarFile = event.target.files[0]
const { data, error } = await supabase.storage
  .from('avatars')
  .upload('public/avatar1.png', avatarFile)
```

```Dart
void main() async {
  final supabase = SupabaseClient('supabaseUrl', 'supabaseKey');

  // Create file `example.txt` and upload it in `public` bucket
  final file = File('example.txt');
  file.writeAsStringSync('File content');
  final storageResponse = await supabase
      .storage
      .from('public')
      .upload('example.txt', file);
}
```

--------------------------------

### Download Storage Buckets Using Supabase CLI

Source: https://github.com/supabase/supabase/blob/master/apps/docs/content/troubleshooting/pausing-pro-projects-vNL-2a.mdx

This snippet demonstrates how to download storage buckets from a Supabase project using the Supabase CLI. It requires logging in, linking to the project, initializing the project configuration, and then copying the storage bucket files recursively to the current folder using the experimental storage copy command.

```bash
npx supabase login

# link to your project
npx supabase link

npx supabase init

# will download files to current folder
npx supabase storage cp -r ss://bucket . --experimental
```

--------------------------------

### SQL: Restrict file uploads to specific extension in a bucket using `storage.extension()`

Source: https://github.com/supabase/supabase/blob/master/apps/docs/content/guides/storage/schema/helper-functions.mdx

This SQL policy restricts authenticated users to upload only PNG files into the 'cats' bucket in Supabase Storage. It checks both the `bucket_id` and uses the `storage.extension()` helper function to verify the file extension.

```sql
create policy "Only allow PNG uploads"
on storage.objects
for insert
to authenticated
with check (
  bucket_id = 'cats' and storage.extension(name) = 'png'
);
```

--------------------------------

### Create RLS Policy for Storage Bucket Access

Source: https://github.com/supabase/supabase/blob/master/apps/docs/content/guides/storage/schema/custom-roles.mdx

Defines a Row Level Security policy that grants full read permissions to the 'manager' role for all objects in the 'teams' bucket. This policy uses the bucket_id to restrict access to a specific storage bucket. The policy can be customized for different access patterns.

```sql
create policy "Manager can view all files in the bucket 'teams'"
on storage.objects
for select
to manager
using (
 bucket_id = 'teams'
);
```

--------------------------------

### Create Storage Bucket and Configure Access Policies in SQL

Source: https://github.com/supabase/supabase/blob/master/apps/docs/content/_partials/user_management_quickstart_sql_template.mdx

Sets up a Supabase Storage bucket named 'avatars' and creates three RLS policies controlling access. Policies allow public read access to avatar images, permit anonymous uploads, and restrict update operations to the file owner, providing secure but accessible avatar file management.

```sql
insert into storage.buckets (id, name)
  values ('avatars', 'avatars');

create policy "Avatar images are publicly accessible." on storage.objects
  for select using (bucket_id = 'avatars');

create policy "Anyone can upload an avatar." on storage.objects
  for insert with check (bucket_id = 'avatars');

create policy "Anyone can update their own avatar." on storage.objects
  for update using ((select auth.uid()) = owner) with check (bucket_id = 'avatars');
```

--------------------------------

### Restrict Storage Uploads to Authenticated Users for Specific Bucket

Source: https://github.com/supabase/supabase/blob/master/apps/docs/content/guides/storage/security/access-control.mdx

Creates an RLS policy that restricts file uploads to authenticated users and limits uploads to a specific bucket by ID. This prevents unauthorized uploads and scopes access to your designated storage bucket.

```sql
create policy "policy_name"
on storage.objects for insert to authenticated with check (
    -- restrict bucket
    bucket_id = 'my_bucket_id'
);
```

--------------------------------

### Configure Supabase Storage bucket for audio files in TOML

Source: https://github.com/supabase/supabase/blob/master/examples/edge-functions/supabase/functions/elevenlabs-text-to-speech/README.md

This TOML configuration defines an 'audio' storage bucket within a Supabase project. It sets the bucket to private, limits file size to 50MiB, allows only MP3 audio types, and specifies a local path for objects. This bucket will be created upon running `supabase start`.

```toml
[storage.buckets.audio]
public = false
file_size_limit = "50MiB"
allowed_mime_types = ["audio/mp3"]
objects_path = "./audio"
```

--------------------------------

### List Files in Supabase Storage Bucket in TypeScript

Source: https://context7.com/supabase/supabase/llms.txt

The `listFiles` function fetches a list of files within a specified folder in a Supabase storage bucket. It supports pagination (`limit`, `offset`) and sorting options. The function returns an array of file metadata, or throws an error if the listing fails.

```typescript
async function listFiles(folder: string) {
  const { data, error } = await supabase.storage
    .from('avatars')
    .list(folder, {
      limit: 100,
      offset: 0,
      sortBy: { column: 'name', order: 'asc' },
    })

  if (error) {
    throw new Error(`List failed: ${error.message}`)
  }

  return data
}
```

--------------------------------

### Create Supabase Storage Policy for Public Uploads

Source: https://github.com/supabase/supabase/blob/master/examples/storage/resumable-upload-uppy/README.md

This SQL command creates a policy in Supabase Storage allowing public users to insert objects (upload files) into a specified bucket. Replace 'your-bucket-name' with the actual name of your storage bucket.

```sql
CREATE POLICY "allow uploads" ON storage.objects FOR INSERT TO public WITH CHECK (bucket_id = 'your-bucket-name');
```

--------------------------------

### Configure Supabase Storage Buckets and Access Policies with SQL

Source: https://context7.com/supabase/supabase/llms.txt

This SQL script sets up two Supabase storage buckets: 'avatars' for publicly accessible images and 'private-files' for user-specific documents. It defines Row-Level Security (RLS) policies for each bucket, controlling who can select, insert, update, and delete objects based on authentication status and user ID. Dependencies include the `storage` schema and `auth` functions provided by Supabase. Policies ensure that users can only manage their own files in private buckets.

```sql
-- Set up Storage bucket
insert into storage.buckets (id, name, public)
values ('avatars', 'avatars', true);

-- Policy: Anyone can view avatar images
create policy "Avatar images are publicly accessible"
  on storage.objects for select
  using (bucket_id = 'avatars');

-- Policy: Authenticated users can upload avatars
create policy "Anyone can upload an avatar"
  on storage.objects for insert
  with check (
    bucket_id = 'avatars'
    AND auth.role() = 'authenticated'
  );

-- Policy: Users can update their own avatars
create policy "Users can update own avatar"
  on storage.objects for update
  using (
    auth.uid()::text = (storage.foldername(name))[1]
    AND bucket_id = 'avatars'
  );

-- Policy: Users can delete their own avatars
create policy "Users can delete own avatar"
  on storage.objects for delete
  using (
    auth.uid()::text = (storage.foldername(name))[1]
    AND bucket_id = 'avatars'
  );

-- Private bucket for user documents
insert into storage.buckets (id, name, public)
values ('private-files', 'private-files', false);

-- Policy: Users can only access their own private files
create policy "Users can access own private files"
  on storage.objects for select
  using (
    bucket_id = 'private-files'
    AND auth.uid()::text = (storage.foldername(name))[1]
  );

create policy "Users can upload own private files"
  on storage.objects for insert
  with check (
    bucket_id = 'private-files'
    AND auth.uid()::text = (storage.foldername(name))[1]
  );
```
